requirement.original: Clean up all auth-flag dead code. The write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py script was already fixed by another session (task 20260526-052559) to remove the auth-flag requirement. But surrounding hooks and cleanup scripts still reference the now-dead auth-flag concept. Remove all dead code.
requirement.clarified: Remove all references to the dead claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag auth-flag concept from 3 hook files (pretool-write-guard.sh, pretool-bash-safety.sh, stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh). Preserve all sentinel (claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*.json) protection code.
requirement.what: Remove dead auth-flag code from 3 hook files
requirement.why: The auth-flag mechanism is dead code -- /commit --bulk uses disable-model-invocation:true so reaching the sentinel writer already proves human invocation. No separate auth flag needed.

included: Remove claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag pattern from pretool-write-guard.sh case block
Remove claude-bulk-allowed- grep condition from pretool-bash-safety.sh entry gate
Remove auth-flag reap section from stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh
Update comments/docstrings to remove auth-flag references
excluded: tests/generated/* (historical generated tests; not acceptance criteria for this cleanup)

success_criteria: grep -rn 'bulk-allowed' across all 3 hook files returns 0 matches
sentinel (claude-bulk-commit-sentinel) protection remains in pretool-write-guard.sh and pretool-bash-safety.sh
constraints: Do not remove or weaken sentinel protection

root_cause_analysis.symptom: 3 hook files still reference the dead claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag auth-flag concept after write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py was already cleaned
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Task 20260526-052559 fixed the sentinel writer script but did not clean up the surrounding hooks that referenced the now-dead auth-flag pattern
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A - dead code left behind by incomplete cleanup
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The auth-flag was the original authorization mechanism before the sentinel-only flow was adopted
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Dead code creates confusion, false guard triggers on a nonexistent file pattern, and maintenance burden
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Auth-flag removed from write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py by task 20260526-052559; hooks not yet cleaned
observations: grep -rn 'bulk-allowed' in hooks/ returns hits in all 3 files (observed: Grep tool)
grep for 'bulk-allowed|auth.flag' in scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py returns 0 matches (observed: Grep tool) -- confirming the script is already clean
stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh:57-61 reaps /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-${SID}.flag which is never created (observed: Read tool)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Grep for 'bulk-allowed' across hooks/ confirmed all 3 files contain the dead pattern. Read of write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py confirmed 0 references to auth-flag remain in the source.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: Not a regression bug -- dead code cleanup after intentional refactor

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Remove all dead code referencing auth-flag from 3 hook files
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: grep -rn -E 'bulk-allowed|bulk[- ]?auth|auth[- ]flag|bulk-commit-auth-flag' across all 3 hook files returns 0 matches
sentinel deny patterns remain in pretool-write-guard.sh and pretool-bash-safety.sh

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook; cycle modifies hook shell scripts only. Does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths.

evidence.observed: 3 hook files still reference claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag auth-flag after write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py was already cleaned by task 20260526-052559

measured.value: grep -rn 'bulk-allowed' returns matches in pretool-write-guard.sh:15,21,22, pretool-bash-safety.sh:637,665,669, stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh:51,57

expected.value: 0 matches for 'bulk-allowed' in any hook file; all auth-flag references removed
expected.source: User requirement: auth-flag concept is dead, sentinel-only flow is correct

evidence.gap: 8 lines across 3 files still reference the dead bulk-allowed pattern

reference_source.description: write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py already cleaned of auth-flag by task 20260526-052559; confirmed via grep returning 0 matches

context.codebase_state: 3 hook files contain dead auth-flag references; sentinel writer already cleaned

development_approach.strategy: Remove dead auth-flag code from 3 hook files; update comments to sentinel-only language
development_approach.validation_approach: grep -rn -E 'bulk-allowed|bulk[- ]?auth|auth[- ]flag|bulk-commit-auth-flag' across 3 files returns 0; grep for 'claude-bulk-commit-sentinel' confirms sentinel protection preserved

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex found 5 issues. 2 applied (expanded AC grep pattern, missed stale comment at line 933), 1 minor wording fix applied, 2 rejected as out-of-scope.

findings.issue: AC1 only checks 'bulk-allowed' but auth-flag/bulk-auth/bulk-commit-auth-flag-write terminology could survive
findings.rationale: Valid -- the user asked to remove all auth-flag references, not just bulk-allowed patterns

findings.issue: Missed stale comment at pretool-bash-safety.sh:933 saying 'script-level gate handles auth'
findings.proposed_fix: Include line 933 in required changes; replace with sentinel-only wording
findings.rationale: Valid -- the script's auth-flag gate was removed by task 20260526-052559

findings.issue: Sentinel preservation ACs are too weak -- grep for string could be satisfied by comments alone
findings.proposed_fix: Add behavioral checks using synthetic hook payloads
findings.rationale: Out of scope -- user asked for dead-code removal, not behavioral regression testing of existing sentinel protection. The grep AC verifies sentinel code was not accidentally removed during editing.

findings.issue: Bare sentinel-writer allowance security ambiguity after auth-flag removal
findings.proposed_fix: Mark as out-of-scope or narrow spec to canonical writer shape only
findings.rationale: Out of scope -- the bare-writer allowance is existing behavior unrelated to auth-flag cleanup. User explicitly excluded pretool-git-privilege-guard.py changes.

findings.issue: tests/generated/ non-goal wording claims tests 'remain valid as regression tests' which is overstated
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to 'historical generated tests; not acceptance criteria for this cleanup'
findings.rationale: Valid minor wording improvement -- some tests specifically test the auth-flag path which no longer exists

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied findings #1 (expanded AC1 grep pattern), #2 (added line 933 to affected changes), #5 (reworded test file non-goal). Rejected #3 and #4 as out-of-scope for dead-code cleanup.
